message:
perf(channel): eliminate per-waiter Arc+Mutex in MPSC channel

Replaces the Arc<SharedWaiter> indirection and per-waiter Mutex in the
MPSC channel with inline waker storage and monotonic u64 identity.

Previously each Reserve future allocated an Arc<SharedWaiter> containing
a Mutex<Waker>.  Every waker access — registration, update (will_wake
check), wake on capacity release, and cleanup on drop — required locking
this inner mutex even though the outer ChannelInner lock was already held
for all operations except the final wake (which clones the waker first).

Now:
- SendWaiter stores the Waker inline alongside a u64 identity tag
- Identity comparison uses cheap u64 equality instead of Arc::ptr_eq
  (which involves pointer comparison on two separate allocations)
- Waker updates happen directly on the VecDeque entry under the outer
  lock — no inner Mutex round-trip
- Reserve tracks waiter_id: Option<u64> instead of waiter: Option<Arc<..>>
- next_waiter_id counter on ChannelInner provides monotonic uniqueness

Additional improvements in the same change:
- SendPermit abort path and Receiver::drop now use SmallVec<[Waker; 4]>
  instead of Vec<Waker> when draining all waiters, avoiding heap
  allocation for the common case of <= 4 queued senders
- SendPermit abort has an early exit when send_wakers is empty, skipping
  the drain + collect entirely
- Receiver::drop waker collection uses .map(|w| w.waker) instead of
  .filter_map(|w| w.shared.waker.lock().ok()...) since there is no
  inner lock to fail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
